pattern: Every divisor's score only depends on how many numbers it evenly divides, so trying each candidate divisor in turn and keeping the one with the highest tally (favoring the smaller value on a tie) picks the winner in one pass.
hints: Each divisor can be scored on its own: count how many of the numbers it divides evenly.
Track the best score seen so far along with which divisor produced it.
When a new divisor matches the current best score rather than beating it, only switch to it if it is smaller than the divisor currently held.

edge_inputs.description: single number, single divisor, no match
divisor far larger than every number, zero matches
several divisors tie at dividing everything, smallest must win
divisor 1 always divides everything and is also the smallest

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(1000, size // 2 + 1))
    m = max(1, min(1000, size // 3 + 1))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    divisors = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(m)]
    return [nums, divisors]
stress.note: small divisor range keeps ties and matches common, exercising the tie-break rule
